data.name: richTextBox1.Text
data.value:
/sap/opu/odata/sap/API_PRODUCT_SRV/A_Product('1020458001')/?$expand=to_Description,to_ProductProcurement,to_ProductUnitsOfMeasure
Product Group: /sap/opu/odata/sap/API_PRODUCT_SRV/A_Product?$filter=ProductGroup eq '7030410'&$select=Product,ProductGroup,Brand,ProductType,Division,BaseUnit,PurchaseOrderQuantityUnit,ProductStandardID&$expand=to_Description
https://SGLSAPS4FEU1.spar.net:44300/sap/opu/odata/sap/API_PRODUCT_SRV/A_Product('1020458001')/?$expand=to_Description,to_ProductProcurement,to_ProductUnitsOfMeasure
@EndUserText.label: 'Flattened Product View' @OData.publish: true define view ZC_ProductFlat as select from mara inner join marc on marc.matnr = mara.matnr inner join makt on makt.matnr = mara.matnr { key mara.matnr as Product, makt.maktx as Description, marc.werks as Plant, marc.mmsta as PlantStatus, mara.meins as BaseUnit }
🛒 Product Story – Explained for a Beginner Company Buyer What is this product?
This product is a Clover Super Milk – Strawberry Flavoured, sold in 300 ml units.
It is a finished, ready‑for‑sale grocery product, not a raw material or semi‑finished item. [sparza-my....epoint.com]
How buyers will recognise it
Internal SAP product number: 1020458001
Primary barcode (EAN): 6001299044519
Brand: Clover
Product type: Finished retail product
Created in SAP: September 2022 (still active) [sparza-my....epoint.com]
This means buyers can confidently use this item in purchasing, replenishment, promotions, and store distribution.
How this product is sold and ordered
From a buyer’s perspective, this product can be ordered and handled in multiple packaging levels: Single unit (each)
Size: 300 ml
Weight: ~320 g gross
Barcode: 6001299044519
This is what the customer buys off the shelf.
[sparza-my....epoint.com]
Inner pack
6 units per pack Useful for stores or short replenishment cycles Own barcode for scanning/logistics [sparza-my....epoint.com]
Case
24 units per case This is the default purchasing unit Case weight: ~8.5 kg Case dimensions: suitable for warehouse handling and palletisation [sparza-my....epoint.com]
➡️ Important buyer insight: When you place a purchase order, SAP expects the quantity in cases, not individual bottles. [sparza-my....epoint.com]
Logistics & warehouse handling
The product has predefined dimensions and weights at each packaging level, which enables:
Accurate transport planning Warehouse slotting
Store delivery optimisation [sparza-my....epoint.com]
No batch management or serial tracking is required, simplifying goods receiving and stock movements. Storage condition is defined (standard chilled dairy handling). [sparza-my....epoint.com]
Quality & risk considerations
This product does not require special quality inspections during procurement. It is not classified as hazardous, not pilferable, and not batch‑tracked. [sparza-my....epoint.com] That reduces both administrative burden and compliance overhead for procurement teams.
Lifecycle & reliability
Created in 2022 and updated as recently as October 2025, indicating the item is still actively maintained. Not marked for deletion, so it is safe to continue buying and ranging. [sparza-my....epoint.com]
✅ What a Beginner Buyer Should Remember
“This is a normal grocery product that we buy by the case, receive by the case, store easily, and sell individually in stores.”
It has:
Clear barcodes Standard packaging No complex handling rules Reliable supplier data
🔍 Suggested Additional SAP OData Calls for a Complete Buyer View To turn this into a fully rounded product story, I recommend supplementing this data with the following APIs: 1. Pricing & costs
To answer: “What does this product cost us?”
API: API_PRODUCT_PRICE_SRV
Data gained:
Purchase prices
Validity dates
Cost conditions
2. Supplier & source of supply
To answer: “Who do we buy this from?”
API: API_PRODUCT_SOURCELIST_SRV
Data gained:
Preferred vendors
Source list validity Procurement rules
3. Sales & listing data
To answer: “Where and how is this sold?”
API: API_PRODUCT_SRV/A_ProductSales Data gained:
Sales organisation listings Item category and sales relevance Distribution channel data
4. Tax classification
To answer: “How is this taxed?”
API: API_PRODUCT_SALES_TAX_SRV Data gained:
VAT classification Regional tax handling
5. Stock & availability
To answer: “Do we have enough stock?”
API: API_MATERIAL_STOCK_SRV Data gained:
Plant‑level stock Available vs restricted quantities
6. Shelf‑life & freshness (if applicable)
If dairy freshness becomes important:
API: API_BATCH_SRV (only if batch management is later enabled)
🧠 Final Recommendation For executive‑friendly or buyer‑friendly reporting, I recommend structuring your output as:
Product summary
How it is bought
How it is sold
Logistics & storage
Cost & supplier
Stock position
This mirrors how non‑technical buyers think and avoids exposing SAP complexity unnecessarily.
If you’d like, I can:
Design a JSON “buyer view” Propose a Power BI / Excel layout
Map SAP fields → business‑friendly labels
Just tell me your preferred output.
